summary: Package name implies Vue 3 binding but ships unmodified maplibre-gl
details: maplibre-gl-vue3@5.24.1 is published under a name that suggests a Vue 3 binding for MapLibre GL, but the package contents are an unmodified republish of the upstream maplibre-gl library: package.json description is the upstream MapLibre description, homepage points to maplibre.org, repository points to maplibre/maplibre-gl-js, the README is the upstream MapLibre README, and the dist/ bundle is the upstream library. No Vue 3 binding or wrapper code is present. The package.json also declares itself as a runtime dependency ("maplibre-gl-vue3": "^5.24.0"), and the README has stray publish-command lines ("pnpm run build-dist", "npm publish --registry https://registry.npmjs.org", "pnpm add maplibre-gl-vue3...") prepended to the upstream content — consistent with a rebadged copy-paste republish rather than an official MapLibre release. No exfiltration, install-time fetch+execute, silent-relay, credential collection, or backdoor mechanism is present in the package; the installer harm is that developers searching for a Vue 3 wrapper get a name-confusing relabel of the base library instead.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
